rationale: The citing paper explicitly cites Spiller et al. (2013) when implementing floodlight analysis via the Johnson-Neyman procedure after finding interaction effects. This is used to compute and report the moderator ranges where labeling effects become significant (e.g., dietary-restraint cutoffs) in key hypothesis tests across studies. The citation therefore plays a direct methodological role in how results are probed and interpreted, not just a passing literature mention.
relation_description: The citing paper applies the moderated-regression probing framework from Spiller et al. (2013), especially floodlight (Johnson-Neyman) analysis, to interpret interactions. It uses this approach to identify specific levels of dietary restraint where branding effects on consumption and physical activity are significant.
